ops.title: ccx vcs ship watches ALL runs on the pushed SHA with per-run report; RELEASED since v0.11.0, brew at 0.13.0 as of 2026-07-12; golangci-lint version:latest breaks main periodically

ops.body:
`ccx vcs ship` (since 976af726, 2026-07-10) watches every workflow run on the pushed commit (`gh run list --commit`, 60s registration retry + post-watch settle re-list, transient-tolerant polling) and prints per-run `workflow · conclusion · duration · url` lines plus a `--budget`-capped (default 2000 tokens) `--log-failed` excerpt on red runs; `CI error` = infra failure after a successful push (summary still prints, exit 1). Live streaming to stderr only on a real tty. A one-shot capt-hook nudge (vcs_guards.py, pack 0.3.0) steers manual `gh run watch` toward ship.

Non-obvious context:
- The transcript investigation behind it: 468 manual `gh run watch` calls vs ~21 ship calls in 3 weeks — the dominant cause was fleet non-adoption, not ship defects; the nudge addresses it. Tag/release runs remain legitimately manual (ship has no tag concept).
- `.github` lint job uses golangci-lint `version: latest` with `only-new-issues: false` — a linter release can redden main on untouched code (v2.12.2 did on 2026-07-10; 7 pre-existing findings fixed in 2f7c6d7d).
- internal/web/fetch.go renders `errors.Join(failures...)` as text (`%s` + `.Error()`) deliberately so `errStealthRequired` never enters the `errors.Is` chain — guarded by two no-leak tests; do not "fix" to `%w`.
- 2026-07-10: cc-skills fragment updated to the watch-all wording (cc-skills 9d162c48; backlog 2477065 closed; fan-out verified in cc-context dc334db and captain-hook) ([[cc-guides-fragment-pipeline]]).
- RESOLVED 2026-07-12: the overhaul shipped in v0.11.0; brew ccx is at 0.13.0 and backlog 52d2a8c is closed. A bare `CI success` line now only means an old pre-0.11 binary somewhere in the fleet.
